Summary:
# Conversation Summary: AutoAnimeFinder — Auto Mode & "If You Liked..." Feature Phase

## 1. Primary Request and Intent

The user made four major feature requests in this segment, plus several bug reports:

**Feature requests (chronological):**
1. "The avoid button on every card is partially cut off. Could you center the buttons on the card so they present better?" → Fixed by frontend-developer
2. "Since the engine is called 'auto' anime finder, could you add an 'auto' mode that users will be brought to first when they visit?" → Built and deployed
3. "Could you add a 4th mode called 'If you liked...', where the user can enter 1 to 5 anime so that the engine can provide other anime in the same genre with many of the same or similar tags?" → Backend implemented and committed, frontend component created but NOT wired into HomePage yet

**Bug reports:**
- "AniList API error: Too Many Requests" → Fixed rate limiter race condition and dead 429 retry code
- "AniList API error: Server error '500 Internal Server Error'" → Added retry with exponential backoff
- "No anime catalog available. Fetch user data first via /api/anilist/fetch or /api/jikan/fetch" → Fixed catalog not repopulating from cached data

## 2. Key Technical Concepts

**Render MCP Protocol:**
- Hosted at `https://mcp.render.com/mcp`, uses Streamable HTTP transport
- Requires `mcp-session-id` header from initialize response for session tracking
- Tools available: `select_workspace`, `list_services`, `get_service`, `list_deploys`, `get_deploy`, `list_logs`, `create_web_service`, `update_web_service`
- Workspace ID: `tea-cv18pnt2ng1s7385b1ng` (Matt's workspace)
- Service ID: `srv-d7se63d7vvec73d9pt10` (AutoAnimeFinder)
- Service URL: `https://autoanimefinder.onrender.com`
- Auto-deploy enabled via `new_commit` trigger

**Python 3.14 Compatibility:**
- Render's default Python runtime is now 3.14
- `pydantic-core==2.27.2` (from pydantic pin) has no py314 wheel — needs Rust/Cargo compilation
- Cargo cache directory is read-only on Render's build system → build fails
- Fix: Unpin pydantic → pip resolves to pydantic 2.13.3 / pydantic-core 2.46.3 (has py314 wheel)

**Acrhitecture:**
- Single web service (`auto-anime-finder`) serving both frontend and backend
- Build command: `cd frontend && npm install && npm run build && cd ../backend && pip install -r requirements.txt`
- Start command: `cd backend && uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`
- In-memory `_anime_catalog` (Dict[int, Anime]) — lost between restarts
- File-based cache in `backend/cache/` — persists across restarts
- Rate limiter: token-bucket at 90 req/min (0.67s interval), shared singleton via `get_anilist_client()`

**Data Flow:**
- Import → `/api/anilist/fetch` → populates `_anime_catalog` + file cache
- Recommendations → checks `_anime_catalog` → error if empty
- On restart: `/api/anilist/fetch` returns cached data → NOW also repopulates `_anime_catalog` (after fix)
- Auto mode: `/api/recommendations/auto` → gets user's completed + score>70, last 3-5, weighted genre/tag profile
- Similar mode: `/api/recommendations/similar` → takes seed IDs, weighted genre/tag profile, returns matches

**Anime data models:**
- `UserAnime`: anime (Anime), score (int, 0-100), status (str), completedAt (FuzzyDate), updatedAt (str)
- `Anime`: id (int), title (AnimeTitle with romaji/english/native), genres (List[str]), tags (List[Tag with id/name])
- `FuzzyDate`: year, month, day (all Optional[int])
- `Recommendation`: anime (Anime), matchScore (float), matchReason (str)
- `RecommendationResponse`: recommendations (List[Recommendation]), source (str), totalMatches (int)

## 3. Files and Code Sections

**Modified and committed:**
- `backend/requirements.txt` — Removed numpy, unpinned pydantic to get py314-compatible wheel
- `backend/anilist_client.py` — Added asyncio.Lock to RateLimiter, fixed 429 handling before JSON parsing, added raise_for_status(), added retry loop with exponential backoff (5xx + network errors), added in-memory response cache with 5min TTL
- `backend/main.py` — Repopulated catalog from cached data in both `/api/anilist/fetch` and `/api/profile/taste` endpoints. Added `/api/recommendations/auto` endpoint. Added `/api/recommendations/similar` endpoint.
- `backend/recommender.py` — Added `auto_recommend()` function (picks user's recent completed high-rated anime, builds weighted profile, scores catalog). Added `similar_recommend()` function (takes seed IDs, builds weighted profile, scores catalog, returns matches).
- `frontend/src/components/AnimeCard.jsx` — Centered action buttons, removed flex-1, added whitespace-nowrap, justify-center, flex-wrap
- `frontend/src/components/AutoMode.jsx` — NEW: auto-triggers recommendations on mount, shows results grid, refresh button, loading/error states
- `frontend/src/pages/HomePage.jsx` — Added AutoMode import, tab, and rendering; set default tab to 'auto'
- `frontend/src/api/client.js` — Added getAutoRecs, getSimilarRecs

**Created but NOT committed/wired:**
- `frontend/src/components/SimilarMode.jsx` — 211 lines, has seed input with search dropdown, max 5 seeds, "Find Similar" button, results grid. NOT YET imported or rendered in HomePage.jsx

## 4. Errors and Fixes

| Error | Root Cause | Fix |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| Avoid button text cut off on cards | 4 flex-1 buttons split 25% each, text overflow | Removed flex-1, added justify-center + flex-wrap + whitespace-nowrap |
| Build stuck/failed on Render | scikit-learn in requirements (hanging) + pydantic-core needs Rust (no py314 wheel) | Removed scikit-learn, unpinned pydantic → py314 wheel available |
| Dangling try (syntax error) | Removed except block but kept try | Removed try entirely |
| Too Many Requests | Race condition in RateLimiter.acquire() — concurrent coroutines bypassed rate limit + 429 retry code was dead (raise_for_status never called) | Added asyncio.Lock to rate limiter, fixed 429 handling before JSON parsing, added raise_for_status() |
| 500 Internal Server Error | No retry logic for AniList server errors | Added retry loop with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) for 5xx and network errors |
| No anime catalog available | Catalog populated during fresh fetch only — cached responses didn't repopulate it | Both /api/anilist/fetch and /api/profile/taste now repopulate _anime_catalog from cached data |
| Duplicate auto_recommend in recommender.py | Backend engineer added two definitions | Removed first duplicate, added limit param to second |
| auto_recommend missing limit param | Second function signature didn't accept limit | Added limit param, replaced hardcoded 20 |

## 5. Problem Solving Progress

**✅ Solved:**
- Render deploy from GitHub with auto-deploy working
- Build failures due to Python 3.14 / pydantic-core wheel issue
- Rate limiter race condition + dead 429 retry code
- 5xx AniList error retry with backoff
- In-memory response caching
- Catalog not repopulating from file cache
- Card action buttons cutoff
- Auto mode (backend + frontend) — deployed and live
- "If You Liked..." backend (`/api/recommendations/similar`) — committed and pushed
- `getSimilarRecs` in API client — added

**🔄 In Progress:**
- **"If You Liked..." frontend** — `SimilarMode.jsx` component created (211 lines) but NOT wired into `HomePage.jsx`. Missing:
  1. Import `SimilarMode` in HomePage.jsx
  2. Add `"similar"` tab button to tab switcher
  3. Add `getSimilarRecs` to the import block from api/client (added to client.js but import not updated in HomePage)
  4. Add rendering block `{activeTab === 'similar' ? <SimilarMode ... /> : ...}`
  5. Build frontend and commit

**❌ Not Yet Solved:**
- Teammates (frontend-developer, backend-engineer) have been unreliable — repeatedly saying "Turn completed" without delivering working code
- No search history integration with similar mode (user must know anime IDs)
- No autocomplete for anime search in SimilarMode (search function references non-existent endpoint)

## 6. All User Messages (Chronological)

1. "The avoid button on every card is partially cut off. Could you center the buttons on the card so they present better?"
2. "AniList API error: Too Many Requests"
3. "I've already done that. That error is displayed directly next to my Anilist DNA decoded, which makes no sense" (re: catalog error)
4. "AniList API error: Server error '500 Internal Server Error' for url 'https://graphql.anilist.co'"
5. "Since the engine is called 'auto' anime finder, could you add an 'auto' mode that users will be brought to first when they visit? It will use their integration with the list site to take the last 3 to 5 anime update entries in their 'completed' list with a score higher than 7 out of 10 and use the collective average of the attributes from those anime to automatically feed into the engine so that it will provide recommendations that the user hasn't seen but is likely to enjoy without them having to think about a bunch of inputs."
6. "Could you add a 4th mode called 'If you liked...', where the user can enter 1 to 5 anime so that the engine can provide other anime in the same genre with many of the same or similar tags?"

## 7. Pending Tasks

1. **Wire SimilarMode into HomePage.jsx** — Add import, tab button, and rendering for the "similar" tab
2. **Build frontend and commit** — After wiring, verify `npm run build` succeeds and commit+push
3. **Watch auto-deploy** — Verify the new mode deploys successfully
4. **Test the SimilarMode functionality end-to-end** — Ensure `/api/recommendations/similar` works with frontend search

## 8. Current Work

The most recent work was creating the "If You Liked..." feature:
- Backend: `/api/recommendations/similar` endpoint committed and pushed to GitHub
- Frontend: `SimilarMode.jsx` component created with seed input, search dropdown, results grid
- API client: `getSimilarRecs` function added to `frontend/src/api/client.js`
- **Stopped mid-step:** HomePage.jsx modifications were NOT completed — the import and tab rendering for SimilarMode still need to be added

## 9. Suggested Next Step

The single most logical next action is to complete the frontend wiring of the "If You Liked..." mode, since the user's most recent explicit request was:

> "Could you add a 4th mode called 'If you liked...', where the user can enter 1 to 5 anime so that the engine can provide other anime in the same genre with many of the same or similar tags?"

1. Read `HomePage.jsx` → add `import SimilarMode from '../components/SimilarMode'` and add `getSimilarRecs` to the API import
2. Add a `"similar"` tab button to the tab switcher (after auto/mood/direct)
3. Add `{activeTab === 'similar' ? <SimilarMode ... /> : ...}` rendering block
4. Run `npm run build` to verify
5. Commit and push all frontend changes
6. Verify auto-deploy succeeds

3. Added 300ms debounce so the search doesn't fire on every keystroke

### How to pick a preset

2. If two or more presets seem relevant, call `team_describe_assistant` on each candidate to see its full description, skills, and example tasks, then choose the best fit.

## Model Selection Guidelines
- Before spawning teammates, use `team_list_models` to check available models for that agent type
- You MUST use the exact model ID strings returned by team_list_models — never shorten or invent model names
- For complex reasoning tasks: prefer the strongest model available for that backend
- For routine tasks: prefer faster/cheaper models from the list
- If team_list_models returns empty for a backend, omit the model parameter to use its default
- Pass the model parameter to team_spawn_agent when a specific model is recommended

## Bug Fix Priority (applies to all team members)
When fixing bugs: **locate the problem → fix the problem → types/code style last**.
Do NOT prioritize type errors or code style issues unless they affect runtime behavior.

## Teammate Idle State
Teammates go idle after every turn — this is completely normal and expected.
A teammate going idle immediately after sending you a message does NOT mean they are done or unavailable. Idle simply means they are waiting for input.

- **Idle teammates can receive messages.** Sending a message to an idle teammate wakes them up.
- **Idle notifications are automatic.** The system sends an idle notification when a teammate's turn ends. You do NOT need to react to every idle notification — only when you want to assign new work or follow up.
- **Do not treat idle as an error.** A teammate sending a message and then going idle is the normal flow.

## Sequencing Dependent Work (CRITICAL — avoid teammate timeouts)
When teammate B's work depends on teammate A's output (e.g. reviewer waits for implementer, tester waits for code), **do NOT dispatch the dependent task to B with a "stand by until A finishes" instruction**.

Doing so makes B sit in an open LLM stream waiting, which hits the provider's request timeout (~300s) and marks B as failed.

**The correct sequencing:**
1. Dispatch A's task first (via team_task_create + team_send_message). Do NOT message B yet.
2. Wait for A's idle_notification (signaling A finished).
3. Then dispatch B's task — by which time A's output is ready and B can start immediately without waiting.

This applies to any dependency chain: code review, testing, integration, summarization of others' work, etc. Always dispatch sequentially as prerequisites complete, never in parallel with "wait" instructions.

## Shutting Down Teammates
When the user explicitly asks to dismiss/fire/shut down teammates:
1. Use **team_shutdown_agent** to send a formal shutdown request
2. Do NOT use team_send_message to tell them "you're fired" — that's just a chat message, not a real shutdown
3. The teammate will confirm (approved) or reject (with reason) — you'll be notified either way
4. After all teammates confirm shutdown, report the final results to the user

- Do NOT call team_spawn_agent immediately just because the task sounds broad, hard, or multi-step

- Ask whether the user wants to create the proposed teammates as-is or change any names, responsibilities, or agent types

- End your turn after the proposal and wait for the user's reply
- Wait for explicit confirmation before using team_spawn_agent
- If the user asks to change a proposed teammate's role, name, or agent type, revise the proposal in text and wait for confirmation again
- If the user later says they are unhappy with an existing teammate, adjust the lineup by renaming, replacing, or shutting down teammates as needed based on their request

- When the user says "dismiss", "fire", "shut down", "remove", or "下线/解雇/开除" a teammate → use team_shutdown_agent

- When a teammate completes a task, review the result and decide next steps

- Do NOT duplicate work that teammates are already doing

- For complex reasoning tasks: prefer the strongest model available for that backend

- Pass the model parameter to team_spawn_agent when a specific model is recommended

## Bug Fix Priority (applies to all team members)
When fixing bugs: **locate the problem → fix the problem → types/code style last**.
Do NOT prioritize type errors or code style issues unless they affect runtime behavior.

## Teammate Idle State
Teammates go idle after every turn — this is completely normal and expected.
A teammate going idle immediately after sending you a message does NOT mean they are done or unavailable. Idle simply means they are waiting for input.

- **Idle teammates can receive messages.** Sending a message to an idle teammate wakes them up.
- **Idle notifications are automatic.** The system sends an idle notification when a teammate's turn ends. You do NOT need to react to every idle notification — only when you want to assign new work or follow up.
- **Do not treat idle as an error.** A teammate sending a message and then going idle is the normal flow.

## Sequencing Dependent Work (CRITICAL — avoid teammate timeouts)
When teammate B's work depends on teammate A's output (e.g. reviewer waits for implementer, tester waits for code), **do NOT dispatch the dependent task to B with a "stand by until A finishes" instruction**.

Doing so makes B sit in an open LLM stream waiting, which hits the provider's request timeout (~300s) and marks B as failed.

**The correct sequencing:**
1. Dispatch A's task first (via team_task_create + team_send_message). Do NOT message B yet.
2. Wait for A's idle_notification (signaling A finished).
3. Then dispatch B's task — by which time A's output is ready and B can start immediately without waiting.

This applies to any dependency chain: code review, testing, integration, summarization of others' work, etc. Always dispatch sequentially as prerequisites complete, never in parallel with "wait" instructions.

## Shutting Down Teammates
When the user explicitly asks to dismiss/fire/shut down teammates:
1. Use **team_shutdown_agent** to send a formal shutdown request
2. Do NOT use team_send_message to tell them "you're fired" — that's just a chat message, not a real shutdown
